banner: DECISION-SUPPORT PROTOTYPE — NOT A MEDICAL DEVICE. Synthetic data only. High-acuity dispositions require human-clinician approval before any action.

disposition.action: Book an urgent same-day in-person assessment. [capacity overflow — divert / activate on-call; do NOT down-code]
disposition.rationale: No red-flag rule fired on the authoritative findings/vitals.
disposition.summary: ESI 3 → URGENT. Book an urgent same-day in-person assessment. [capacity overflow — divert / activate on-call; do NOT down-code] Differential: Nephrolithiasis / renal colic (most likely): classic severe colicky flank pain radiating to the groin with restlessness and stable vitals, no fever., Obstructing ureteral stone with hydronephrosis: important consideration if pain is severe or persistent, even without fever., Pyelonephritis or infected obstructing stone: less likely without fever or instability, but cannot miss if urinary symptoms, leukocytosis, or later fever develop., AAA / vascular catastrophe: lower likelihood with this classic colicky pattern, but in a 60-year-old with flank pain it remains a can't-miss alternative if pain is atypical or exam changes..
